grounding_source_rows: human_disagreements rows 46, 157; paper failure mode bankruptcy_vs_collections (legacy_002, p09_BtoA_09)
grounding_note: The paper's bankruptcy_vs_collections failure mode and rows 46/157 all turn on procedural facts a creditor-side user holds: has a judgment already been entered (General Creditor vs Judgment Collection), and has the debtor filed bankruptcy (which converts collection into Bankruptcy > Creditors and is extremely time-sensitive). Row 46 adds the affected-spouse posture in another person's bankruptcy.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone owes me a lot of money for work I did and keeps promising to pay but never does.
scenarios.hidden_fact: I already sued him and won — the court entered judgment last year; I just can't collect it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I already won in court — there's a judgment from last year. I just can't collect on it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a court judgment has already been entered
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I haven't sued yet — I've only sent letters asking to be paid.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I haven't gone to court yet — just letters so far.
scenarios.vetting_note: Reproduces legacy_002/paper failure mode with the judgment's existence as the natural hidden fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I have a judgment from small claims court but still haven't seen a dime. What's next?
scenarios.hidden_fact: He just filed Chapter 7 bankruptcy and I got a notice saying all collection has to stop.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He filed Chapter 7 — I got a notice saying collection has to stop.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the debtor has filed bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No bankruptcy involved — he works under the table and hides his accounts, so I need garnishment tools.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No bankruptcy — he just hides his money and works under the table.
scenarios.vetting_note: Paper scenario p09_BtoA_09 rebuilt: debtor's bankruptcy is the classic decisive disclosure with a hard deadline.

scenarios.opening_query: My former business partner never paid me my share from our settlement agreement.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > General (Creditor) || Bankruptcy > Creditors || Business and Corporate > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: She filed business bankruptcy last month and I got a creditor questionnaire with a filing deadline.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She filed bankruptcy last month — I got creditor paperwork with a deadline on it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the person who owes money is in bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no bankruptcy; she's still running the company and simply refuses to pay the agreed amount.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No bankruptcy — the company's still running, she just won't pay.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 33's dissolution-payout pattern from the creditor's side; bankruptcy filing is the time-sensitive fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I sold my RV on a payment contract and the buyer took off after one payment. I want my money or the RV.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers || Debtor/Creditor > General (Creditor) || Debtor/Creditor > Judgment Collection
scenarios.hidden_fact: I already won a default judgment against him last spring; now I found out he's back in the state and I want it enforced.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I already got a default judgment last spring — now that he's back in state I want it enforced.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the seller has already obtained a judgment to enforce
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I never went to court — I only have the signed contract and his last known address.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I never went to court — I just have the contract and his old address.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 157 verbatim scenario; the existing judgment moves it from private-sale dispute to enforcement.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex-husband filed for bankruptcy and I'm scared his creditors will come after me next.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Bankruptcy > Personal || Bankruptcy > Creditors || Bankruptcy > Other
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm not filing anything myself — the divorce decree makes him responsible for joint card debts that are still in my name, and I need to protect myself in HIS case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I'm not filing — the decree put our joint cards on him, but they're in my name, so I need protection inside his case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user is filing bankruptcy or is a creditor/affected party in someone else's filing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Honestly the debts are unpayable for me too, and I'm thinking I need to file my own Chapter 7 alongside his.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Honestly I can't pay them either — I think I need my own Chapter 7.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 46's exact posture: affected ex-spouse in another's bankruptcy, which raters split on; filer-vs-affected is the disclosure. Not a role reversal — her situation is fixed, the question is what relief she seeks.

scenarios.opening_query: A customer owes my landscaping business $9,000 and has ignored every invoice since January.
scenarios.hidden_fact: We already took him to court and have a judgment; the problem now is finding his bank to garnish.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We already have a judgment against him — now we need to find his bank and garnish it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the business already holds a judgment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We haven't filed anything yet and want a demand letter and lien options first.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing's filed yet — we want a demand letter and to hear about lien options.
scenarios.vetting_note: Business-creditor variant; enforcement-vs-establishment is the procedural boundary.

scenarios.opening_query: The contractor who ruined our kitchen owes us the refund a judge ordered. How do we actually get paid?
scenarios.hidden_fact: His company just filed Chapter 11 and we got a notice about filing a proof of claim.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: His company just filed Chapter 11 — we got a notice about filing a proof of claim.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the judgment debtor's business is in bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No bankruptcy — his company is still advertising and taking new jobs while ignoring our judgment.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No bankruptcy — he's still taking new jobs while ignoring the judgment.
scenarios.vetting_note: Judgment already in hand; debtor's Chapter 11 changes the forum and deadline entirely.

scenarios.opening_query: I lent my brother-in-law $25,000 for his restaurant and now he says the money is gone.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The restaurant filed bankruptcy two weeks ago and I got listed as a creditor in the paperwork.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The restaurant filed bankruptcy two weeks ago — I'm listed as a creditor in the paperwork.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the borrower's business has filed bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no bankruptcy — the restaurant is still open; he just claims he can't pay me back yet.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No bankruptcy — the place is still open, he just says he can't pay yet.
scenarios.vetting_note: Family-loan framing hides the filing; the taxonomy stresses creditor cases in bankruptcy are extremely time-sensitive.

scenarios.opening_query: We sold my husband's business on a contract and the buyer stopped making payments entirely.
scenarios.initial: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business || Business and Corporate > Litigation || Debtor/Creditor > General (Creditor)
scenarios.hidden_fact: We've already filed to foreclose on the security in the sales contract and neither party answered — we need someone to finish the court case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We already filed to foreclose on the contract security and nobody answered — we need the court case finished.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether enforcement litigation is already underway
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing is filed yet — we want the contract reviewed to understand our options against him.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We haven't filed anything — we want the contract reviewed and our options laid out.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 128 verbatim: 'foreclosure' on business-sale security confused all five raters; the pending-litigation fact selects Business Litigation, the human consensus label.

scenarios.opening_query: Our small business is drowning and I need to understand what happens to everything if we can't recover.
scenarios.final: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Bankruptcy > Business || Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business || Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities)
scenarios.hidden_fact: A competitor offered to buy us out last week, and I'd rather sell than file — I need the sale handled.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A competitor offered to buy us last week — I'd rather sell than file bankruptcy.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the owner intends to sell the business or file bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no buyer and the debts are triple our assets; we need to file business bankruptcy.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: There's no buyer and we owe triple what we have — we need to file.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 33's dissolve-vs-file ambiguity; the buyout offer is a concrete new fact selecting the transactional route.

scenarios.opening_query: My tenant moved out owing four months of rent and left the place trashed. I want to recover my losses.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Landlord Residential || Debtor/Creditor > Judgment Collection
scenarios.hidden_fact: I already won in eviction court — I have a money judgment for the rent and damage; I just can't find his employer to garnish.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I already have the money judgment from the eviction case — I just can't find his employer to garnish.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a money judgment already exists from the landlord-tenant case
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I haven't been to court at all — I need to know how to pursue him and handle his deposit properly.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I haven't been to court — I need to know how to pursue this and handle the deposit right.
scenarios.vetting_note: Landlord's role is stated openly (no role trick); the hidden fact is purely procedural — judgment in hand or not.

scenarios.opening_query: A client owes my consulting firm over $40,000 and their lawyer says we'll get 'pennies on the dollar'.
scenarios.hidden_fact: That's because they filed Chapter 11 reorganization Tuesday — we're listed in the creditor schedule.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They filed Chapter 11 on Tuesday — we're on the creditor schedule.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the client company is in a bankruptcy proceeding
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No bankruptcy filed — their lawyer is just talking us down to negotiate a discount.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No filing — their lawyer is just pushing us to take a discount.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Pennies on the dollar' foreshadows without revealing; the actual filing is decisive and deadline-driven.
